work_unit: AS-EC-017 fix: autonomous-loop.sh --bg process group leak
situation: audit-2026-07-02-002 finding AS-EC-017 (priority 17, pending): autonomous-loop.sh line 26 trap uses `kill 0` which signals the entire process group. In --bg mode, nohup child inherits parent's process group; when user Ctrl-C/kill triggers the trap, `kill 0` can hit unrelated processes in the same group (e.g., the launching terminal session). Additionally, run_round runs claude -p synchronously with no PID tracking, so the trap has no precise target.
action: Three-part fix: (1) Replace global `trap 'kill 0' INT TERM` with named `_cleanup` function that only kills ACTIVE_ROUND_PID (the current claude -p child). (2) Add `setsid` before nohup in --bg branch so backgrounded loop becomes a new session leader, fully detached from launcher's process group. (3) Refactor run_round to background claude -p, capture PID into ACTIVE_ROUND_PID, then `wait`; clear PID after shred cleanup. Syntax-checked with bash -n. Committed via opt-worktree.sh (direction-shift auto-detected due to public-interface-adjacent engine script change).
outcome_evidence: bash -n syntax check passed; opt-worktree.sh committed and pushed auto/opt-engine-shift-1782939897 to origin; worktree diff shows 3 hunks applied correctly (_cleanup function, setsid nohup, run_round PID tracking); main untouched (restore confirmed by system-reminder post-commit).
next_suggestion: 派生 AS-EC-018 fix (opt-worktree.sh realpath -m resolves symlinks causing path rejection) — last remaining pending finding from audit-2026-07-02-002. After that, all derived_fixes will be merged/rejected → set audit-cycle-state.status=cycle-complete to trigger next full audit.
